candidates.title: Post-release hardening and observability cleanup

candidates.safety_boundary: No runtime code; only docs and reference corrections.

candidates.title: Backtest/report dashboard usability follow-up
candidates.summary: Improve dashboard empty-state messaging, add export timestamp headers, and improve backtest summary table column alignment for Markdown/HTML output.
candidates.user_value: Reviewers get more polished, professional report output with clearer empty-state guidance.
candidates.safety_boundary: Dashboard remains read-only, local, and has no execution controls or external assets.
candidates.ranking_reason: Continues the v0.6.10 dashboard polish trajectory; small, self-contained.
acceptance_criteria: Empty-state dashboard renders a clear message instead of blank output
Export includes timestamp header
All existing dashboard tests pass
Dashboard remains read-only with no execution controls

candidates.title: Broker/provider preflight diagnostics without enabling execution
candidates.summary: Add read-only diagnostic output to atlas doctor showing configured (but not activated) broker/provider settings, credential presence checks (without revealing values), and connection-readiness hints.
candidates.user_value: Users can diagnose configuration issues without accidentally triggering execution.
candidates.safety_boundary: Read-only diagnostics only. No broker connections opened. No provider API calls made. No orders placed. No credentials logged.
candidates.ranking_reason: Reduces user confusion about configuration without crossing execution boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: atlas doctor shows broker/provider config presence without revealing credentials
No network calls made during diagnostics
check_submit_execution_safety.py passes
No live execution triggered by diagnostic output

candidates.title: Paper-trading workflow documentation and safe examples
candidates.summary: Create or refresh docs/paper-trading-guide.md with step-by-step paper-trading setup, safety reminders, and annotated example configurations. Add a demo script showing paper workflow.
candidates.user_value: Users and reviewers can follow a safe, documented path to paper trading.
candidates.safety_boundary: Documentation and example configs only. Paper mode remains gated and does not execute live orders.
candidates.ranking_reason: High user-value docs improvement; no runtime changes.
acceptance_criteria: docs/paper-trading-guide.md exists with step-by-step instructions
Guide includes safety reminders and risk disclaimers
No live trading enabled by documentation or example configs
check_public_docs_consistency.py passes

candidates.title: Release/checker simplification after v0.6.10
candidates.summary: Consolidate or archive version-specific release checkers from v0.5.x/v0.6.0-v0.6.8 era into a historical directory. Reduce the number of active release scripts without losing audit trail.
candidates.user_value: Faster CI, less confusion for new contributors, cleaner scripts/ directory.
candidates.safety_boundary: Archival only; no active checker logic removed. Historical evidence preserved.
tests_checks: git log to verify no evidence deleted
candidates.ranking_reason: Reduces maintenance burden; medium risk because it touches many files but only moves/archives them.
acceptance_criteria: Historical scripts are archived, not deleted
Active release checkers (v0.6.10, v0.6.11) remain functional
CI and dev_check still pass
No historical evidence is lost

candidates.title: Test-suite performance and generated-artifact hygiene
candidates.summary: Profile slow tests, skip or mark known-slow tests for targeted runs, and ensure generated artifacts (.atlas/backtests/) do not accumulate unboundedly in CI or dev environments.
candidates.user_value: Faster local development cycle and cleaner CI runs.
candidates.safety_boundary: No runtime behavior changes. Test-only and hygiene improvements.
candidates.ranking_reason: Useful but not blocking; can be done incrementally.
acceptance_criteria: Slow tests are identified and marked
Generated artifacts do not accumulate unboundedly
All existing tests continue to pass
No runtime behavior changes

Provider execution remains disabled by default.

Risk gates remain deterministic and unchanged.

No provider APIs, broker APIs, or real webhooks are called from CI or local checks.
